title: tocoyena costanensis
intro: Tocoyena costanensis, also known as the forest tocoyena or Costanea tocoyena, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Native to Brazil, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Tocoyena costanensis include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
